intro: Diasperus pohlianus, also known as Brazilian ironplant or Brazilian iron tree, is a flowering plant native to Brazil, belonging to the family Leguminosae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anemia, due to its high iron content, which provides several health benefits such as improving red blood cell production and reducing fatigue. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus pohlianus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support iron deficiency and anemia treatment.
